reviews.review_description: I’ve reed over some of the reviews  posted . It is understandable that some Inner World members will not have the same experience . I can only speak to my own experience with Inner World before it was called  XR Health. I was having a very hard emotional time and found this world on my VR headset. My experience was wonderful. It is amazing concept where you can enter in anytime and seek support and  I would say 9 times out of 10 people just need someone to listen and HEAR what they’re dealing with I went into a few weekly “meetings’ and it was wonderful how random users were so supportive and empathetic. I’ll end w this I truly believe this concept/program could save a life- I had a very hard childhood and of course I have  emotional health issues.  The last time I shared I encouraged Inner World to keep going.  So many people struggle w thoughts of hurting themselves or others and picking up a headset and feeling heard and loved for 5 minutes could truly change the outcome of choices made during those times of crisis and heartaches. Keep going IW you are changing lives

They promote mental illness, the guardians or watchers. I felt some had unusual behavior and some were passive. Some are eavesdropping issues, I do have the ability to sense. Got banned because I had a drive conversation with a friend in a world that no one was there. Triggered my anger to its maximum because the approach was rude, suddenly.. the guardian appears and I had the cleanest record, she started saying things and it was during my time in the hospital (spent 6 months) and I was a daily always there paying member. Innerworld helped me immensely but getting a ban.. in that way.. and I don’t get to see all the friends I made me pray everyday that it needs to shutdown or someone has to buy them off, I would’ve. I am Hue.

Innerworld is a great place to relax, a great place to learn, to better yourself, and a great place to socialize and have fun.

They have lots of meetings and events, from learning to cope with anxiety/addictions or deal with other emotions, to playing Pictionary by drawing in 3-D, or relaxing guided meditation, and even sometimes just hanging out with others running around in a world just creating art with the colored pen tools drawing everywhere just having silly fun.

People are kind and understanding, and actually care about you as a person. Innerworld is better than any other VR community I have tried.

2023-07-08: after the recent changes to navigation, teleport mode is almost unusable for me. When I report about how much of a problem it is for me just to walk around, I get told “it’s by design” and that REMOVING left hand navigation is an “upgrade.”

Do better. At least consider how negatively it affects some users to remove such an integral feature.

2023-07-10:
I did reach out via helpdesk, and that's where I got the response.

The teleport mode does NOT work the same, and it's not an upgrade.

It's hard (for me) to draw and move around at the same time using the same hand. The old mode allowed using both hands. Left for navigation and right for drawing. This was great, and was consistent with controls in most games.

In most games, left hand is standard for movement, and right hand for interactions. Right hand nav is great for those that can use it, but removing the ability to move via left stick is a MAJOR downgrade in usability for those of us that have difficulty moving and interacting with the same hand at the same time (like drawing for Pictionary).

2026-02-01:
I'm disappointed the dev team doubled down on a non-inclusive design choice -- I want to be clear, this is an intentional choice to remove a fundamental navigation control, but only for teleport.

Left-handedness isn't a mere preference or disability.

Removing left-hand nav is non-inclusive.
signing up an acct wasnt a smooth experience but worth the patience it took. Friendly, helpful staff and relateable community. First event I went to was really well done. Seems like a safe space.
I love the kinds group therapy feel to the app it is worth checking out if you need a support system i docked a star because i can't talk about spiritual stress without being censored
I think this app is a good fit for people that cbt can help.

Update on my review, I have since been removed from the app under false accusations, I wish all my past friends a happy and healthy life. If you get to see this review, please take care, feel free to reach out to me here to stay in touch. Much love to you all. Pinkyloo :) 

I cannot tell you how much this place has
 changed my life. The guardians, guides, and admin are all fantastic. The members are so supportive. The support I have received throughout the ups and downs has been awesome. 

The negitive reviews here show nothing towards the app, just from angry ex members who were unpleasant towards staff and members.

Im too nervous to use this app haha im a very shy person and i dont like speaking to people online so i cant use this app it looks like a very good app for my struggles but thats the one thing keeping me away from using it

I first entered The commons where I met kitty a guardian we all hang out play games and draw and talk there is always a really kind balanced guardian to make sure things stay within respectful boundaries it took me a while to go to the meetings they have been so helpful. I feel I’ve found my tribe. Also I love the flying .Thank you Noah  - Chloe

Please  don't close the comz  it's turned


my life around  for the best it's got me mixing  more and it's help me to make new friends  on there  I love kitty  she the best and art nut  and  its help me to stop stress  Please  keep the comz

No thank you, we were imply having a conversation about our parents who are sick or died and the pain. With the medical system in helping them. Basically, it was a banned Covid. I understand that it could be political, but that is part of the trauma that many of us face. I’ll see keep seeing my therapist.

developer_response.body:
Hi AtmosphericGirl,

Thank you so much for taking the time to share your experience. We're truly sorry to hear that your conversation felt censored, especially when you were discussing something so personal and painful. We know that grief, trauma, and medical hardship are deeply human experiences, not just topics.

We genuinely want Innerworld to be a place where people can feel heard and supported, even when the subject matter is tough. If you're open to it, we'd love to hear more about what happened so we can understand better and try to make it right. You're not alone, and we deeply respect your vulnerability in sharing this. 💙

Please feel free to reach out to us at helpdesk.inner.world if you'd like to talk more.

reviews.review_description:
I had high hopes for Innerworld, especially given its initial promise of offering free support groups for mental health issues like depression, anxiety, grief and loss, etc. The free tier provided some great opportunities, with various groups focused on therapeutic practices like DBT. This made me optimistic about signing up for the on year subscription, which cost $119 USD.

However, my experience quickly soured when I encountered a simple issue: a spelling mistake in my username. When I reached out to customer support, I was met with outright refusal to assist me. The representative would not clarify which part of my username was misspelled or provide any instructions on how to fix the issue. As a result, I was unable to access the platform I had paid for. Despite multiple attempts to resolve the situation, I was left stranded without support and had no choice but to cancel my subscription.

After revisiting the platform, it’s clear that Innerworld has undergone a drastic shift. The subscription price has skyrocketed to $80 USD per month, which amounts to $960 USD for the year (or $1,386.43 CAD for me as a Canadian), compared to the $119 USD annual fee I paid just over a year ago. The free tier has been severely limited, with only a couple of mental health support groups available—currently, there’s a group for healing from narcissistic relationships and a veterans’ group, but both only meet once a month, making them difficult to access. The remaining free content is comprised of talent shows, drawing groups, and a single addictions group, none of which focus on actual mental health support. Crucial mental health groups like those for anxiety and depression are now hidden behind a steep paywall, making them virtually inaccessible, especially for those who are low-income.

It’s frankly despicable that a platform that once claimed to offer accessible mental health support is now capitalizing on people’s vulnerabilities at such a steep price. In today’s political climate, where mental health issues are more pressing than ever, it’s deeply troubling to see a service that once provided free help turn into a profit-driven model that prioritizes financial gain over actual support. This platform has gone from a potential lifeline to a costly barrier for those who need help the most.

I will not be recommending Innerworld to anyone, and I will be looking for alternatives that genuinely care about people’s mental well-being rather than exploiting it.

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We are really sorry to hear that things didn’t go as you hoped. Your frustration is completely valid, and we genuinely regret that we let you down, especially at a time when you were seeking support.

We also deeply apologize for the trouble you had with your username and the lack of clarity from our support team. That’s not the experience we want anyone to have. We’ll be taking this feedback seriously to make sure we improve how we support our members moving forward.

Regarding the pricing, we totally understand how the shift feels disappointing, especially compared to what was previously available. As Innerworld grows, so do the costs of running the platform and keeping the lights on, and that’s part of why we've had to adjust our model. That said, we absolutely don’t want finances to be a barrier to getting mental health support.

If you or someone you know is struggling with the cost, please don’t hesitate to reach out to our helpdesk. We’d be more than happy to see what we can do. We’ve helped others access the platform at reduced or no cost based on their circumstances, and we’d be glad to try to help you too.

And if you ever feel like giving Innerworld another shot, the door is always open. You’re more than welcome here.

Wishing you all the best,
The Innerworld Team 💙

reviews.review_description: Has anyone noticed how expensive this app subscription has become? This app clearly only wants to help people’s mental health who have a lot of money. This is not accessible. It’s extremely depressing to see that absolutely nobody low income or even average income could actually justify afford doing this app, especially when they tell you it needs to be in combination with therapy which anyone that’s in therapy knows how expensive that is to begin with as well. The people that have produced in our world should be absolutely ashamed of themselves.

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Thank you for sharing your concerns. We genuinely appreciate your feedback and understand that cost can feel like a barrier. Innerworld was created to help as many people as possible, and we continually work to make the platform more accessible.

If you haven’t already, we encourage you to check out our free tier to see if there’s a version that fits your needs. We absolutely hear your frustration, and your comment is a vital reminder that there’s always more we can do to reach people with tighter budgets.

Please feel free to reach out to our support team at https://helpdesk.inner.world/hc/en-us if you’d like to talk about pricing in more detail—we’d love to see if there’s a solution that could work for you. Thank you again for taking the time to share your thoughts; your feedback helps guide us as we grow and improve.

reviews.review_description: Just a another cash grab app for mental health and it’s not even run by professionals who have been trained in how to give advice. Nobody ever gets better so you just pay each month £30 for useless meetings after meeting. I’d give it no stars if I could. Just a playground for grownups who spend all day every day just hanging around.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your honest feedback. We’re really sorry to hear that your experience with Innerworld didn’t meet your expectations.

To clarify, Innerworld isn’t meant to replace therapy or professional mental health care. We’re a peer support community grounded in evidence-based tools and our guides are trained in how to use those tools to create safe, supportive conversations, though they’re not licensed therapists. We always try to be upfront about that.

That said, we completely understand that Innerworld isn’t the right fit for everyone. While we’ve seen many people experience real growth, connection, and healing here, we also know that everyone’s mental health journey is different, and what works for one person may not work for another.

If you ever choose to give the platform another try, we’d be happy to help you find groups that might feel more meaningful or helpful to you. And if the cost is ever a concern, you’re more than welcome to reach out to our helpdesk, we’ll do our best to support you however we can.

Wishing you nothing but the best on your journey.

reviews.review_description: Edit i am trying it again but Rec Room is better

reviews.review_title: Very nice and great people so far !!
reviews.review_description: Just joined and was very welcomed by many individuals. Played a game called mind reading or something like that and it was really a nice icebreaker and fun! I love this app, sadly it costs 80$ a month after the 7 day free trial. But if your on disability like I am , please reach out to them regarding a payment plan , they have a scholarship fund for people on a fixed income, so you can get all the great support  you need. I’m so happy to finally went into the app to explore, you will not be disappointed with all the many groups and games/ events it has to offer ! Very awesome!!! Highly recommended.

Innerworld is not just a game. Sure, there's similarities, but it is more of a social app where anyone can go and get support from the kindest, most empathetic people I have ever met. However, as is the case with everything in this world, where people are involved, there will be those who are toxic. There's people on here leaving comments calling out Innerworld guides and members. Ignore them. They are butthurt because they were banned for being toxic and malicious. Innerworld takes this kind of thing very seriously, and I had to learn the hard way who needs to be avoided. For the most part people are genuine and supportive. 

If you're depressed, lonely, suffering from anxiety or any kind of mental health issues Innerworld is for you. 

Since I started using Innerworld things have started to improve for me. What seemed like a hopeless situation, suddenly seems not so hopeless after all. Thanks to Innerworld I feel I've found some self worth, and I no longer feel so alone. There's always someone there available to talk to, and for someone like me it has been life saving. I can't say enough good things about this app.

The guides are all great, and the warmth and love you get from the community has brought me out of a deep depression. The groups are incredible and there's something for everyone. 

I actually look forward to jumping in and seeing some familiar faces, well their avatars. I never thought I'd get much out of it because I am extremely anxious and never talk to people online, but the people here made me feel so welcome. I found the courage to speak and that says a lot about how great this community is, The toxic people aside. Let's  not let petty haters ruin what is, altleast for me a fantastic support network.

Please consider trying Innerworld. Even if you don't speak, just listen. I promise you, you don't have to be on your own anymore. We're all here and looking forward to meeting you.

Terrible on onboarding experience. First you have to create an account, then answer a long list of very intimate questions only after which the app reveals for the first time that you need a premium account to use it which requires a pricey subscription. No information upfront. These are dark e-commerce patterns in an app supposedly about mental health. Also the graphics are crap.
Innerworld is an amazing app if you are looking for someone to talk to or just to hang out. Everyone is friendly and supportive.
Can you help me to reset my password

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Please reach out to us at helpdesk.inner.world and someone from our team will be able to help you with this!

reviews.review_description: ITS ABSOLUTELY WORTH IT
I love this app,and the people,groups,and places in here are amazing. Thank you

Cool concept, but just not many people showing up and I think the price point for membership has a lot to do with that. The tools are great. Very informative app. It definitely feels like group therapy despite being constantly reminded (probably for legal reasons) that this app is NOT therapy. I think this app is the future of mental health services. Looking forward to seeing sliding scales or having insurance reimburse memberships. A lot of people have a great need for this.

Innerworld is full of positive energy and I love that all people are so supportive caring loving . They never judge others and I never ever see a negative energy anywhere in here . Inner world is such a beautiful place to visit it healing my heart with love kindness joy and happiness ❤️ and all the guardian and guide they are so so sweet I love you guy so so much ❤️
Extremely stuttery (on Quest 2) for such a simple scene. And requires setting up an account, including creating a password, which is tedious unless you don't care about insecure passwords.

I was a member for several months. In the beginning, I liked the meetings on various topics such as anxiety or depression. The exchange with the others was helpful. 

Then I had suggested several times to make meetings possible with topics that were important to me and others, but nothing ever came about and there was no feedback. In addition, it was difficult to have personal conversations in the Commons as soon as you had deeper conversations about problems, a guide came so close that he could listen in without taking part in the conversation, this had a controlling effect on me and made me feel very uncomfortable about it. 

I was then told I shouldn't talk about other apps, such as Beat Saber, that would not be allowed, although every guide has a different opinion about it, which leads to a great deal of uncertainty. I was brought to a guide several times because I had talked to another member about religion and would have used the F*** word several times but we were out of earshot of the guide. So I asked where he knew all this and that's when he said that there is an AI-supported alarm system in Innerworld that notifies all guides who and where something forbidden is currently speaking, and what exactly is forbidden was never really defined but it is always a matter of interpretation by the guides. Every conversation is completely recorded that everyone who uses IW should be aware of that!!!

It was all too much too much ambiguity for me too many uncertainties in dealing with each other too much control and paternalism!!! No thanks I've had that too many times in my life!!

Then guides suddenly disappear and this is not made transparent Especially stupid if you had a good connection with the guide and had developed trust something like that doesn't work if you are interested in creating a community in IW then it is ultimately more business than community!!!
$299 a year? NOPE.

reviews.review_title: Not what it used to be
reviews.review_description:
Let's just say that Innerworld is not what it used to be... A place of healing, tight knit community that felt like family, a safe space where like minded adults would gather to support one another without bias or judgement, and an admin team that was in touch with the community members and respected our opinions and actually appreciated our feedback. 

Now it's more like an over priced place of repetitive information that is readily available for free online, the admin no longer connects with the members and God forbid if you happen to associate with another member outside of the app. It's sad to see such a wonderful thing be used to abuse and take advantage of their employees, volunteers, and paying customers. I pray that others who seek support in this app are not subject to the same traumatizing experience I had after so much progress made healing only to be set back and 86ed for socializing. 

Had it not been for the connections made with other members  from me socializing, the trust factor in being vulnerable and willing to share my own thoughts would have never been possible. I highly respect the educational value behind the tools within the app, however, with lived experience, I also know first hand that it takes more than just a controlled environment that's over seen by educated professionals in mental health, respectfully, to gather individuals that are seeking support, and there actually be substantial progress in their healing.  Fortunately, there is another platform that has actually been extremely helpful in offering  support as well as the freedom to be an integral part of the growing community without sacrificing individuality or violating the members civil rights. I wish all of IW the best of luck in reframing its procedures and protocols to better suit the needs of its users and not just the benefit of the company

developer_response.body: Thank you for considering Innerworld. Our top priority is creating a safe and supportive community for mental health. To achieve this, we have developed comprehensive community guidelines with the help of world-leading clinical psychologists. When someone repeatedly violates these guidelines, despite multiple warnings, we must take action to protect our members. We believe these guidelines are essential for fostering a positive environment where everyone can thrive. We value your feedback and are always working to improve our platform.

reviews.review_description: An excellent app for support for life’s challenges or a quiet place to hang out.  It truly is a welcoming community that doesn’t allow unruly behavior.

When I first joined, for free, they gave me a free upgrade to premium membership. When I went to my account to see what it would be to continue on with premium it said $14.99.

After the two week membership expired I found that all the classes were premium, except for one a day. Having decided to pay the $14.99 price I went to my account to upgrade to premium. It now said $299.95 annually. I clicked on monthly and the price was $40. Their inner circle, in which you can have a group session with a real therapist, is $120 a month.

I feel like I've been played so I'm uninstalling it and deleting my account.

developer_response.body: Hello! We had a pricing change recently but grandfathered our existing users in. If you reach out to us at Helpdesk.inner.world we'd be happy to help you further.

reviews.review_description: First time going to innerworld. I'm a recovering addict trying to get myself together in a healthy way. There was one kid who came in during session and he started talking down to the woman that has extreme anxiety he was immediately banned and everyone came together even closer together. I definitely would recommend this to anyone looking for help in any department. It seems like a great place to let go and finally deal with issues.

Before discovering Innerworld, I felt as though I was wandering through a vast, empty desert of isolation, completely unaware that solace was merely an arm's reach away. Innerworld served as my oasis, providing a sanctuary where I could connect with others who truly understand the depths of my experience. It's a place where healing begins by simply knowing you're not alone.
So my son was playing it and he said something and this old woman said you sound a little young to be play this app and I was casting at the time.

developer_response.body: Hello! Due to the nature of our mental health app, we do currently require people to be 18 or older to use our application. This helps to keep our groups safe and also the child, who may overhear things that are not appropriate for children. We hope you’re able to give Innerworld a try as an adult. If you have any additional questions please feel free to reach out to us at helpdesk.inner.world

reviews.review_title: Looks good but I haven’t played it
reviews.review_description: it’s looks good but it’s laggy for me lol but 5 stars it works really well

This is my favorite app! I have more friends on this amazing peer support/ mental health app than I do in real life. Everyone is anonymous and navigates as cartoon avatars,  so you can be who you really are with no pretense. Trolls are banned as soon as they are discovered.  Members and staff are caring and supportive, and there is someone there to hang out with 24/7.  They offer a wide array of improvement classes in topics like ADHD, autism, veteran support, health, anxiety, and others. You can even hang out and play Pictionary,  Uno, and other group games. They are about to launch support groups led by certified counselors! Can't wait for that! There is a free level and a paid level. Both are wonderful and have plenty of offerings. There is an area for adults and another fully supervised area only for 13-18 year olds. There are always new features and classes rolling out. Cannot speak highly enough of Innerworld! Also available for PC and various phone models. I know there is some sort of iOs support, but am not sure about that. This app only supports English speakers... for now.

I think this app has evolved a lot since its inception, has done lots of great things, lots to offer, and much to grow. It’s so easy to share from your home and feel heard, as well as listen to others. It really helps you feel heard and not alone.

I found Innerworld right after getting my Quest 3 for Xmas and it has been life changing for me. I find the peer to peer perfect for me because people on there have experienced the same things as me. I feel like I am heard and they are sympathetic.

Along with the nurturing environment, they have tools to use that can help with a wide range of challenges that we face in life. The meetings range from serious topics such as narcissistic abuse or anxiety to lighter meetings to play games or getting to hear stories - there are 200 meetings at all hours each week.

There is something here for everyone!
This is just a Clique app and I wish I was kidding. do not recommend

developer_response.body: Hello! Thank you for your feedback on Innerworld. We are an inclusive community where folks from many different backgrounds are accepted without judgement. If you have any concerns or would like to share any feedback, please reach out to us at helpdesk.inner.world

reviews.review_description:
There has been no meetings for a few months, And i'm in a teen acc, That means I can't.
Just join in a world without a meeting.

developer_response.body: Hello! We have temporarily paused our Innerworld Teen product. If you'd like to receive a notification when it is back online, please feel free to submit a request here: https://helpdesk.inner.world

reviews.review_title: Mental wellness is vital these days
reviews.review_description: This app has a little potential to be great if they would spend some time keeping people safer and on a more structured path, not telling people with "real" issues to keep it light. They like to ban people that are not fulfilling their values but I must say the CBI tools are great and they do a good job teaching them. Everyone learns and it's 24hrs a day. Class were always fun and educational, that's about all I got. Enjoy and be safe!

I love this app and the people in it very helpful and educational. I've met people and friends in here that have been down the same path as me. It's very convenient especially when I can't physically make a meeting Love the people that I've met in these rooms.

reviews.review_title: I gave it a 1/5 and I’ll tell you why
reviews.review_description: I hate this game because it’s a MASSIVE RIPOFF OF REC ROOM AND META HORIZON WORLDS, ALSO THE LOADING SCREEN TAKES A VERY LONG TIME, I ain’t playing this ever again until this game improves how long the loading screen will take

I’m sad to have to change my original review, included below. I wrote it in my first two months when it did seem to be an amazing community. Unfortunately, this isn’t a community at all in fact, they greatly discourage real community from being formed. They banned members for talking with other members and behave in many ways that are traumatizing to people that have experienced lots of trauma. The majority of people who have written these reviews have since either been banned, or moved on. Many guides have also been banned as well. I caution anyone from using this app. Please seek out real mental health support from a therapist or psychiatrist. This functions much more like a cult. I have made lifelong friendships though but only with having to have significant battles with innerworld.  Nothing about what I’ve just said should be occurring in a mental health platform.

—
First review & what innerworld could have been if it had let people heal with each other:

When I was reading the reviews of this app, I couldn’t believe how wonderful it sounded. After interacting in it for about a month now, I can say that everyone of them are true! This is the most amazing, supportive community I’ve ever encountered. And they’ve already helped me so much.💕

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your honest feedback about Innerworld. We're sorry to hear that your experience has changed since your initial positive impressions. It's important to us that our community understands Innerworld's focus as a mental health platform, distinct from general social apps. We have specific community guidelines in place, crucial for maintaining a safe and supportive environment tailored to mental health support. These were developed with a committee of clinical psychologists from institutions including Harvard and Vanderbilt. These guidelines, along with our adoption of standard clinical practices, are essential in shaping our approach, though it's important to note that Innerworld is not a substitute for professional therapy.

Regarding the concerns you raised about member bans, please know that we value confidentiality and thus cannot disclose specific reasons for such actions. However, we offer a fair chance for appeal and encourage adherence to our guidelines. We understand and respect that Innerworld may not be the right fit for everyone, and we encourage individuals to seek the most suitable support for their needs, including therapy or psychiatric help. Your input is invaluable to us as we strive to improve and create a supportive community for all members.

reviews.review_description: But then took over £300 out if my account in 1 year

developer_response.body: Hello! Please reach out to helpdesk.inner.world to inquire about refunds. We do not charge 300 pounds for our subscription, so this may be an error on Meta Quest subscription side. We look forward to helping you with this!

reviews.review_description: I've  had to restart the app 3 times, and still can not get time to allow revisions of my avatar. It is so glitchy that even while writing this review, I am unable to see the cursor and text as I write. If I didn't  before, I will need counseling after this

developer_response.body: Hello! We're sorry to hear you've had trouble with the app. If you submit a ticket at helpdesk.inner.world we'd be happy to troubleshoot to help you figure out how to revise your avatar.

reviews.review_description: I paid full membership and NEVER passed or crossed beyond the  Lobby. Customer Services is useless. Right now the app doesn’t recognize my password  nor my username. And automatically appears a message that I am under age . The so called “ support staff “ are just avatars with pre recorded messages.  I am trying to get my money back IF I  someone replies to my emails.

developer_response.body: Hello! We are sorry to hear you're having difficulties with accessing our support. We actually provide support through our Helpdesk which can be access at helpdesk.inner.world. If you let us know the problem, we can help with the refund process and also troubleshoot the issues you're experiencing.

reviews.review_title: Don't throw stones in glass houses
reviews.review_description: First of all I asked for my account to be deleted. Second, I wasn't going to be nice to someone who was lying through their teeth about also leaving cryptic messages with the ToS section. Keep lying to everyone, see where it gets you . You keep banning anyone who disagrees. You've even banned people with no explanation so don't give me that crap.

After reinstalling a few times,  I could never get past the first section where you read a Quick Tip. Nothing else appears. After reading other reviews,  I think I dodged a bullet not being able to get in.

developer_response.body: Hello! We are sorry to hear you're experiencing technical issues. Please reach out at helpdesk.inner.world and we're happy to help solve these issues so you can experience our application

reviews.review_description: I wanted to feel better because I am super depressed and I was going to try inner world instead I feel worse than before. I attempted to use it because instead of me actually being able to experience it, I was left alone with no instructional video on how to walk or talk other than a 5-second dude that didn't help me and I am extremely frustrated because I can't even simply know how to navigate in the game so it just printing much made me absolutely feel worse and frustrated so thanks!

developer_response.body: Hello! We're sorry to hear you had a confusing time here. We have a tutorial that teaches you to navigate the application step by step, including how to move around and speak. If you run into any technical challenges please feel free to reach out at helpdesk.inner.world and we're happy to help you further!

reviews.review_description: This used to be good however the community guides kicked me out just for talking about horizon worlds with a friend, I have not been back since and will not ever go back 😞😓 wad very disappointed the way staff treat you.

developer_response.body: Hello Foxy! We are sorry to hear that you were not following the community guidelines. Due to the nature of our app, we do not allow folks to recruit onto off platform interactions. This is to protect safety, reduce the risk of any harm and maintain anonymity of our members. If you'd like to fill out our appeal form to acknowledge our community guidelines and have a chance at re-entering Innerworld, please feel free to contact us at Helpdesk.inner.world.

reviews.review_title: Not true to its objective has no purpose
reviews.review_description:
Sadly innerworld does not do what it says it does. It has very strict guidelines that completely stop you for talking about anything meaningful and engaging. Not only that they even stop you from talking about other apps, politics, or anything that provocative or engaging.. 

Sadly the admins are hypocrites they say you can be yourself when in reality you can't..

While the app has alot of potential Sadly its not true to its purpose it seems to be more interested in making money and its own interest then really tackling the problem of mental health and helping people... 

The only real benifit is the social aspect and the safe space as its not a gaming app in any way so now that I've said that  the app loses all value.. the app also has an eerie feel to it, you feel half safe half as if you have a thousand eyes silently judging you.

I have been singled out and felt rejected because of very suttle behaviour changes that I've noticed while using the app and interacting with the users and they never tell you directly what the problem is!

For example..

 I've tried to engage in meaningful conversations that just get dismissed for litterally no logical reason. Despite them being very educational and well intended..


I've tried to create positive quotes and art that get deleted the minute I leave the room. While for other stay up unopposed....


When I enter innerworld where before I was greeted with a "hello welcome" I am now ignored with an eerie silence....


I've said to the guide "bambi" that I enjoy speaking with her.. to my shock I never saw her again! 


Some of the reasons I joined the app was to improve my social skills engage in conversations with different people around the world. 


And hopefully be in a community where I would be accepted. 


Unfortunately the guides and moderators that govern this app have not allowed me to achieve that goal.


Not once was I asked about how i feel or was any attempt made to help me feel engaged or improve my mood instead there is a form of suttle bias..  thats very damaging where others are treated completely different to you. 


However the strange behaviours and actions despite being very suttle have made it very clear I'm not welcome on the app.


I'm very confused they say the app is a safe space to help people that "you can be yourself"  and "express how you feel" but I feel unwanted disliked and rejected despite doing my best to abide by the many strict guidelines implemented on the app and be kind loving and constructive. 

I'm very very sad because I believe the app had a good cause and the potential to help many people.  what's worse is I now feel even less confident and more insecure then before.

When I tried to confront them about what i was experiencing I was banned with no faire explication.

developer_response.body: Hello! It sounds like you've had a difficult experience and we'd love to hear more about how we can help you improve your Innerworld experience. In general, we only ban people if they repeatedly break our community guidelines. We also have an appeal form that allows individuals a chance to come back into the community if they're able to acknowledge what community guideline was broken and commit to following the guidelines. Please feel free to reach out to us at helpdesk.inner.world if you haven't already, and we can learn more to try and improve your experience. We also host regular town halls which are an excellent place to give feedback to our admin team.

reviews.review_description: It's rough 'round the edges, and thin in the middle, but I think they're trying, and for now, at least, it's more about the folks that visit or volunteer there than the shine on the things they can otherwise do. I suppose my one huge concern would be that for a site that discourages any religious content (a policy that I endorse) many of their activities and presentations actively incorporate religious and spiritual practices, including adherence to the teachings of various shamans. I know there is a flip side to this and that lots might argue that they're not political enough, or not it's offshoot, religious enough. Maybe, as they grow, there is even room for all of that, but these religions, or quasi ones, would be designated as by their political factions.

reviews.review_title: Pretty sure the good reviews are fake
reviews.review_description: First day I couldn't go anywhere. It just froze every time. Gf and friend had the same experience. Second day I went into a world. Was asked how old I was. Answered and got thrown out. Now I am banned. Btw, I am well over 18. To anyone thinking of getting this, I’d suggest anything else. This app is pretty empty anyway, you can’t be with your friends, and if the admins don't like you, then I guess you get banned? Also, I read they are using the data you provide them for some research company, which is how they get more money. If this is true, don't let them use you as a guinea pig.

developer_response.body: Hi Aiyu_u thanks letting us know about this. We've released a hotfix to the performance issues that resulted from our previous update. In terms of your age, due to the mental health nature of our app, we have to be careful not to allow adolescents to interact with adults. If you'd like to create a ticket at Helpdesk.inner.world we can verify your age and unban you. Lastly, in terms of research - we do not sell data or profit from research. Instead, we conduct research to measure the impact of our intervention and know whether or not it's working. Only through measuring our impact can we improve the clinical intervention over time to help as many people as possible.

reviews.review_description: The Admins follow you around everywhere and monitor everything you say they won’t even let you make a friends and share Facebook stuff. If you are willing to they will kick you out. If you even try to there is a very cold like atmosphere in this place there isn’t anything really special about anyway unless you have a friend to play with, but God forbid you make a connection with one you didn’t know and try to make friends outside of inner world.￼

developer_response.body: Hello! It sounds like your account was banned due to sharing your real life name and requesting others do the same. Due to the mental health nature of our app, our community guidelines include maintaining anonymity to protect each person in the community. If you'd like to appeal the ban please feel free to reach out to us at Helpdesk.inner.world

reviews.review_description: i cannot express how much i love the community here. everyone is so patient and understanding and non judgemental and i just love them all so much!!! please get innerworld if you can because it changed my life

This is a research program. Ethically they should be compensating you, even if that simply means free use. Instead they charge more than apps with actual content. I joined a guided meditation that was a really boring recording. The host literally left and no one was there by the time it was done. They use your data or kick you out of the app, and force surveys. I wonder if all the five star reviews are from staff because there was no one participating…like at all. The place was empty. Don’t give away your time, data, and participation for free.

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for your feedback! We understand that you have concerns about Innerworld and we apologize if your experience did not meet your expectations.

First, we want to clarify that Innerworld is focused around live events, which average 10-15 people per event and are led by live guides. We have a lot more than just meditation - you can see our 100+ weekly events here: https://app.inner.world. These include things like learning cognitive behavioral tools, coping with depression/anxiety, learning to be assertive, ADHD support, and more.

Your feedback on the pre-recorded meditation is helpful. We developed the recordings so people can meditate or learn concepts when a meeting is not happening. The recordings are not designed to be the sole content of our guides during groups, and we will speak to our guides to ensure your experience doesn't happen again.

Second, we are a mental health support service that offers live, guided support groups for individuals struggling with depression and anxiety. Our guides are trained from a new method developed by by a world renowned expert in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, from Vanderbilt University. We do ask for surveys, similar to how some places delivering mental health intervention may collect surveys to know if the intervention is working or not. Our data actually shows 30% reduction in symptoms of depression and anxiety in our users. 

We do also charge a fee for our service, as we believe that mental health support should be accessible and affordable for everyone. While some apps may offer free content, we believe that the value of live, guided support is worth the investment. We also pay all of our guides to facilitate meetings.

We value all of our members and we do not kick them out of the app without cause. We may ask for feedback through surveys, but we always respect our members' privacy and data.

Thanks again for your feedback, as it will help us to continue improving the application. If you'd ever like to try it again, please feel free to reach out to us at Helpdesk.inner.world.

reviews.review_description: a community of mostly caring people who are there for each other it’s real life people dealing with real life non crisis issues and supporting each other using dbt tools and heart. Please note people on this app have real challenges as real people do..establish clear boundaries let the person u are speaking with know if u have triggers to avoid..

reviews.review_title: I couldn't figure any of this out.
reviews.review_description: I haven't had any apps require an in-app login when you visit, which I immediately forgot and didn't want to go through the tedious process of entering with the controller.  When in the app, I watch the intro material, but I couldn't figure out what you were supposed to do and it was very glitchy with menus stuck inside walls, etc.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review of Innerworld. We apologize for any difficulties you experienced while using the app. It sounds like you had some trouble with the login process and navigating the app.

We understand that it can be frustrating to have to log in to an app, but we require a login for the safety and privacy of our users. We want to ensure that each person's personal information and conversations are kept secure within their own group.

As for the glitchy experience you had while using the app, please know that we are always working to improve the user experience and fix any issues that may arise. If you could please contact our support team with more information about the specific issues you encountered, it would be greatly appreciated and we can set up a time to walk you through more components of the application. You can do so at Helpdesk.inner.world. 

We value all feedback and appreciate you taking the time to let us know about your experience with Innerworld. We hope that you will give the app another try and that you have a better experience in the future. Remember, Innerworld is a peer support group app, and the more people who use it, the more helpful it will be for everyone.

reviews.review_description:
This app is not just an app, it's a world full of incredible and kind individuals, an escape out of daily hardships, a community full of tools, support and empathy.
 
I can say that to me innerworld is my second house, a safe space for whoever is seeking support and understanding.

The range of meetings and topics discussed here is wonderful and sharing with others made me learn so many things about myself as well.
It's hard to explain in words how much this app helped me in my mental health journey.

The worlds are beautiful and the qualified guides are there to make sure this world stays safe and protected for all.
If you are struggling and looking for a space that can assist you in your journey then seek no further.

reviews.review_title: A Peer/Group Support and Socializing App
reviews.review_description:
I have tried this app a few times when it was still on App Lab... The basic idea is that they have events with guides that encourage discussion around mental health topics and do group activities. (One activity I tried was writing my life's strengths and weaknesses and writing a measurable-achievable-realistic action to improve each aspect of life, i.e. work, health, fun, etc.) And you can also just join a world and just talk to other people. Everyone was respectful and the app is moderated, so there are no yelling or rude people there. They also has some mini games to play with others.

As for the negatives, I don't like the fact that all audio data is recorded, but they make it abundantly clear at the start and say that they use it for mental health research without selling it. It does help on the moderation side and it would be unfair to deduct a star for that because they're upfront about it and users accept it willingly. Another thing is that the app could use a lot of quality of life features (you can choose push to talk, or just put your finger on the button to talk, but it would've been nicer to also have a button to toggle push to talk without going to the menu). But again, they seem to update frequently and it would be so easy to add minor stuff like that in the future.

All in all, if you're the type of person who might benefit from group support, then it might work for you. I tried it out of curiosity, so I can't be sure how it would affect others in other situations. And I'm giving it 5 stars because less feels like I'm judging it for things that don't really affect its main purpose, even though it doesn't mean it's perfect for everyone.

As a very shy person I find the community safe and welcoming.  I  have learned something new from every group I attended. Great place to reinforce a variety of skills. I find Innerworld to be a great adjunct to my therapy. 

Congrats Innerworld
I'm sure it's a lovely app, but it said it was free, and when you open it you have to buy a subscription. So not free.

I bought my meta quest to solve my social phobia and talk to people. Unfortunately thats not easy for me, i tried the popular social games, but i always quit the game because im too anxoius. I dont have this problem with innerworld because i know, the people i will meet there, have the same struggles and are like me.
Finally i find a game which can help me overcome my fears.

Warm community, wise coaches, beautiful landscapes and music - all of this makes inner world great place to be. Just being yourself, have a support and give it to other people - is precious! 

Thanks to developers and all of you, who is here.

If you're looking for an outstanding experience, Innerworld is just for you! This amazing community makes it easier to go through the day, and there's literally no other place like this. I highly recommend Innerworld!

Okay this is the best app out there.  Innerworld is not only just an app it’s a community of great people who welcomes everyone and treats them with respect.  The events are amazing. We laugh and even cry together but that’s just how close we are.  There is a platform that address mental and physical aspects.  The great thing about Innerworld is that there’s always something going on.  If you believe me, just try it out. You won’t be disappointed.  I highly recommend.

Missytee

Innerworld (former HelpClub) was pretty much the first app I used on my freshly purchased Quest 2 - back then still via ShadowPC/Steam. It quickly became clear to me that the atmosphere here is very different from that in many social apps, where only crazy kids are on the go. Here you can talk calmly with nice people and just be "completely normal". This showed me once again what potential VR has if you only find the right apps.

I myself don't use the approach of finding psychological support here. But I find it an excellent idea for all people who may not have so much family or friends to be able to discuss personal difficulties or get support.

In this respect, it's nice that Innerworld is now officially available for the Quest 2 and I'm very happy to comply with the request to write a review here and support the developers with it! I wish you much success - especially in Europe, so that maybe someday people who don't speak English will feel comfortable here, too.
Amazing app! The community is great, the events helpful. Highly recommended for those struggling with mental health issues.
A place full of comfort and other good emotions. Feels so good to connect with people and get some advices
Mental health and personal growth, available to all in a safe environment, enabling you with effective tools and a supportive community. What's not to like?

reviews.review_title: The most beautiful VR experience so far.
reviews.review_description: Within the first few minutes of using the app I met a member who was very nice to me and introduced me to one of the guardians. We talked a little and they invited me to a meeting, I told them I was nervous (I struggle with social anxiety), and they told me it was ok If I didn't want to talk. However, I felt so inspired by listening to other members that I was able to share a little bit about me and I felt very welcomed. I had been wandering in other VR social apps trying to meet people, since I thought it would be easier for me to socialize in VR, but suprise! social anxiety translates pretty well to this  VR environment, so, to find a safe place where people understand you might be struggling with something in life, makes it so much easier to open up.  It's been 4 months since I downloaded the app and I've attended a bunch of meetings, including one about social anxiety, and I have learned so much from the guardians and other members, not to mention there are other activities you can engage in just for fun. This place has truly inspired me to look inside myself and it has helped me set in motion a lot of positive changes in my life. The worlds are visually stunning and there's a sense of community that makes the whole experience feel like a dream.

I was glad to have found this app. The chats and group classes had in there were very helpful

Thanks 

I’ll be back soon I’m sure 😂 

OddlyRobbie
What a tool to have to work on yourself. The people in the community are so friendly and kind and the guides are so helpful and knowledgable.  Great app great tools great community.

What more can I say? It is an absolute delight to use this app. not only did it give me the opportunity to express my inner feelings, but it also connected me to like-minded people and allowed me to engage in fun activities such as pictionary, trivia, and unplugged. This has really improved my mood and mental health.  I would like to give a big thank you to the developers.
I Iike being able to share personal thoughts in a comfortable space. I'm a bit shy so this helps.

reviews.review_title: I bet it is good  but you have to log on
reviews.review_description:
It is not that cool
I bet it is cool

When I first downloaded this, I really didn't expect much, lately there is not a lot of support for behavioral health challenges in things like Apps. I was seriously Amazed! I am a first responder and BH Specialist, I wanted to explore this app to meet others, and learn more about others challenges, what I found was, a place I could go when my world is upside down, get so much support, and leave feeling so much better than when I came in. No doubt a life changer!!
I love Innerworld. I love our group meetings. I love everyone in there. So supportive, kind, caring. Really is a great place to go

I am so thankful I stumbled upon Innerworld when I first bought my quest. This world has opened me up to beginning to heal in ways I never dreamed imaginable. I love all the guides and have made real friendships in Innerworld. Thank you, Noah, for giving us this immersive cognitive therapy!

As a mental health support app this place is great. Everyone is really nice here and it helps to have supportive people to talk to and try to help you think and go in the right direction. There are a lot of events available about all kinds of topics and this pretty much offers something for everyone. For me it is really difficult to talk to people irl and express myself. The VR part here really helps me overcome hurle to talk, take notes and advice on issues I'm dealing with and how to work that into my life. I'm really happy that this app exists and can help me and others.
